positive_description: Very subtle effect concentrated in the lower face. At +3.0 there is a marginal change in lip area volume or chin contour — the lower face may appear fractionally fuller or the chin slightly more rounded. The upper face is unchanged. At +1.5, indistinguishable from neutral.
negative_description: At -3.0 the lower face shows the opposite marginal shift — possibly slightly thinner lips or a fractionally more pointed chin. The effect is at the edge of visual detection.
symmetry_notes: Appears bilaterally symmetric. No left-right asymmetry detected in the 3/4 views.
artifact_notes: No artifacts at any tested value. Very low vertex displacement throughout.
prose: Beta25 produces barely detectable changes in the lower face, possibly affecting lip volume or chin contour. The renders across the full range show faces that look nearly identical, with any differences confined to the mouth and chin region and marginal enough to be uncertain. Like its neighbors in the beta20s, this component captures fine population variation that explains very little total shape variance. It has no practical utility for expressive face recipes and would require weights far exceeding normal usage to produce visible effects. It could contribute as one of many small components in an identity differentiation scheme.
